reasoning:
1) Base rate and FRED tool anchoring: The FRED tool data showed the last observation (for the week ending Oct 22) spiked sharply to 3038.9, up from 3031 a couple of weeks prior. The tool's linear expectation for Target 1 (1 week away) is 3038, noticeably below the threshold. Coupled with a history of Q4 stagnation, the tool outputs an anchor of 36% for the immediate next observation, which I use as my baseline.

2) Trend and Medium-Term Dynamics: Despite short-term mean reversion, the series enjoys a solid positive drift of approximately +1.15B per week over the rest of 2025 due to an accelerating rate-cut environment improving bank loan metrics and encouraging commercial real estate (CRE) activity. Thus, expected values drift positively as the time horizon extends.

3) Calculations per Target:
- For Target 1 (1 week out, to 2025-11-02), the time series is just coming off an abnormally high outlier, and end-of-month effects historically show slight mean reversion in this series. P is modeled around 38%.
- For Target 2 (4 weeks out, to 2025-11-25), the positive drift (+4B to +5B) overcomes the gap created by short-term reversion, increasing the expected value safely past 3040. Allowing for variance (std ~ 6.8), P rises to 66%.
- For Target 3 (13 weeks out, to 2026-01-24), accumulated drift yields an expected level well over 3050. The confidence is very high, offset sparsely by a tail risk of an unexpected macro slowdown affecting CRE. P is 85%.
